system.name: VentureForge
system.description: A hierarchical multi-agent system with reflection loop that mines pain points from multiple community sources (Hacker News, Product Hunt, Tavily web search, Reddit), clusters similar complaints with LLM-based grouping (1-10 evidence sources per pain point), generates startup ideas, and produces pitch briefs. Uses binary yes/no rubrics for subjective evaluation. Runs on AMD MI300X via vLLM.
system.shared_state: VentureForgeState (Pydantic, see src/state/schema.py). Revision tracking is per-pitch via revision_counts: dict[str, int] keyed by idea_id.
system.revision_scope: Per-pitch: only the specific pitch brief that failed critique is revised. For pitch_writer revisions, only that brief is regenerated. For idea_generator revisions, only that idea and its downstream artifacts are removed and regenerated. For pain_point_miner revisions, all pain points are regenerated (since they're shared across ideas) but the system tracks which idea triggered the revision.

agents.name: Orchestrator
agents.description: Central supervisor that routes tasks to worker agents in sequence, manages shared state, and handles the reflection loop based on critic feedback. Never generates content itself.

input.state: VentureForgeState (full shared state object; see shared_state)

output.state_patch: dict of updated fields (e.g., current_stage, next_node, critique, revision_counts)

agents.name: Pain Point Miner
agents.description: Scrapes multiple community sources (Hacker News, Product Hunt, Tavily web search, Reddit) for genuine user complaints in the target domain. Uses LLM to cluster similar complaints into pain points, each with 1-10 evidence sources (verbatim quotes + URLs). Can be re-invoked by reflection loop if critic finds weak evidence.

input.max_pain_points: integer (default: 30, represents number of clusters, not individual complaints)

rubric.has_verbatim_quote: yes|no (all quotes in evidence must be verbatim)

tools.name: reddit_json_scraper
tools.notes: Requires Reddit API approval (not currently active)

agents.rubric_purpose: Filter out fabricated or weak pain points before downstream agents see them
agents.clustering_strategy: LLM groups similar complaints into pain points. Each pain point has multiple evidence items. Prefer clusters with 2+ sources over single-source complaints.

agents.name: Idea Generator
agents.description: Groups pain points into themes and generates distinct startup ideas. Each idea must address at least 2 pain points. Can be re-invoked by reflection loop if critic finds ideas weak.

agents.name: Scorer
agents.description: Evaluates each idea using a binary yes/no rubric across 3 dimensions (feasibility, demand, novelty). Final score is the count of yes answers. No subjective floating-point scores.

scored_ideas.verdict_logic: park if yes_count <= 2 OR any fatal_flaw.severity == fatal; explore if 3-5 AND no fatal; pursue if >= 6 AND no fatal
scored_ideas.one_risk: string (single biggest risk in one sentence)

agents.name: Pitch Writer
agents.description: Writes investor-ready one-page pitch briefs in markdown for the top N ideas. Can be re-invoked by reflection loop if critic flags hallucinations or tone issues.

input.scored_ideas: array (top N, sorted by yes_count desc)

agents.name: Critic
agents.description: Adversarial reviewer that evaluates pitch briefs using a binary yes/no rubric with 5 core checks (reduced from 7 - removed manual outreach requirements). If any required check fails, returns 'revise' verdict with the target worker agent to re-invoke. Caps at 3 revisions.

input.pain_points: array (for evidence verification - each pain point has evidence array with multiple source_urls)
input.scored_idea: object (Scorer output for this idea, for cross-reference)
input.current_revision_count: integer (0, 1, or 2; Orchestrator caps at 3 per idea_id)

rubric.all_claims_evidence_backed: yes|no (every factual claim traceable to pain point evidence)
rubric.no_hallucinated_source_urls: yes|no (every URL in evidence_links appears in pain_points evidence arrays)
rubric.target_is_contained_fire: yes|no (specific named reachable community, not demographic)

critique.all_pass: boolean (true if all 5 checks are yes)
failing_checks: array of strings (which rubric items returned no)
critique.target_agent_logic: priority 1 pitch_writer if no_hallucinated_source_urls fails (invented URLs); priority 2 idea_generator if positioning failure (target_is_contained_fire or competition_embraced_with_thesis); priority 3 pitch_writer if all_claims_evidence_backed fails but URLs are real (tone down unsupported claims); priority 4 pitch_writer for writing/tone only (tagline)
critique.revision_feedback: string (specific instruction for the target worker)

loop_config.approval_logic: approved if all 5 rubric items are 'yes'

agents.rationale: Removed YC-style manual outreach requirements. Ideas can now pass with strong online community launch strategies (HN, Product Hunt). Focus shifted to evidence quality and positioning clarity.

llm_serving.production: vLLM on AMD Instinct MI300X via ROCm

infrastructure.cache: diskcache for external HTTP APIs (Hacker News + Product Hunt + Tavily + Reddit), TTL = cache_ttl_hours (default 24h)

design_principles.binary_rubrics_over_scores: All subjective evaluation uses yes/no checks rather than 0.0-1.0 scores. Final scores = count of yes answers. This makes evaluation reproducible and auditable.
design_principles.no_hallucinations: Every pain point and pitch claim must cite a real source URL. Critic agent verifies this in reflection.
design_principles.graceful_degradation: Failed agents don't crash pipeline. Errors logged, partial state returned.
design_principles.immutable_state: Agents never mutate state in place. Always return new state via model_copy(update=...).
design_principles.provider_agnostic_llm: Single env var switches between OpenAI/OpenRouter/AMD vLLM. No provider-specific code in agents.

success_criteria: Critic reflection loop triggers at least once during demo
All pain points have real, verifiable source URLs
Gradio UI shows live agent execution with rubric outcomes
Final demo runs on AMD vLLM endpoint (not OpenAI)
Total runtime under 10 minutes per full run
